agent-errors.description: Daily agent execution error counts. SDK: `new Agents(sdk as never).getErrorsTimeline(startTime, endTime)` → `{ data: [{date, value, name}] }`. dataSelector: `result?.data ?? []`, xKey: date, yKey: value

defaults.title: Agent Error Rate
defaults.description: Daily error counts — spot spikes early

invocation-volume.description: Agent AGU consumption over time. SDK: `new Agents(sdk as never).getConsumptionTimeline(startTime, endTime)` → `{ data: [{timeSlice, aguConsumption}] }`. xKey: timeSlice, yKey: aguConsumption

top-failing-agents.description: Agents ranked by error count. SDK: `new Agents(sdk as never).getTopErroredAgents(startTime, endTime)` → `{ data: [{name, count}] }`. template: ranked-table

active-agents-kpi.description: Count of agents with at least one run. SDK: `new Agents(sdk as never).getAll(startTime, endTime)` → `{ items: AgentListItem[] }`. For kpi-card: return `[{ count: result?.items?.length ?? 0 }]`, valueField: count

defaults.title: Active Agents
defaults.description: Agents with at least one run

agent-latency.description: P50/P95 execution time. SDK: `new Agents(sdk as never).getLatencyTimeline(startTime, endTime)` → `{ data: [{name: 'P50'|'P95', value, date}] }`. Pivot by date for multi-line-chart

defaults.title: Agent Latency
defaults.pivotExpression: rawData.reduce((acc: Record<string, unknown>[], row: any) => { const existing = acc.find((r: any) => r.date === row.date); if (existing) { existing[row.name] = row.value; } else { acc.push({ date: row.date, [row.name]: row.value }); } return acc; }, [])

job-failures.description: Faulted jobs filtered with Jobs.getAll({ filter: "State eq 'Faulted'" }) → { items: [{processName, state, createdTime}] }. template: data-table

defaults.title: Faulted Jobs
defaults.description: Jobs currently in a faulted state

job-completion-trend.description: Successful jobs with Jobs.getAll({ filter: "State eq 'Successful'" }) → { items: [{processName, state, endTime}] }. template: data-table

jobs-duration-threshold.description: Jobs running longer than threshold minutes. SDK: `new Jobs(sdk as never).getAll({ filter: "State eq 'Running'" })` then filter by computed duration `(endTime-startTime)/60000 > threshold`

jobs-by-state.description: Jobs filtered by execution state. SDK: `new Jobs(sdk as never).getAll({ filter: "State eq '${params.value}'" })` → `{ items: [{processName, state, createdTime}] }`

tasks-by-status.description: Action Center tasks filtered by status. SDK: `new Tasks(sdk as never).getAll({ filter: "Status eq '${params.value}'" })` → `{ items: [{title, status, priority, createdTime}] }`

defaults.title: Tasks by Status
defaults.description: Action Center tasks filtered by status

cases-running-above.description: Maestro cases where runningCount > threshold. SDK: `new Cases(sdk as never).getAll()` then filter `runningCount > threshold`

defaults.title: High-Volume Cases
defaults.description: Processes with running case count above threshold

hardRefuse.reason: The platform tracks AGU units, not currency.
Per-agent CPU/memory is not exposed by any API.
Job records carry no end-user identity.
hardRefuse.alternative: T3-SDK: new Jobs(sdk as never).getAll({ filter: "ProcessName eq 'x'" }) grouped by processName

hardRefuse.reason: Dashboards are scoped to one tenant.
Raw counts only — no SLA metadata in any API.
hardRefuse.alternative: T3-SDK: new Jobs(sdk as never).getAll({ filter: "State eq 'Faulted'" }) to compute failure rate

hardRefuse.reason: No aggregation endpoint for error text or stack traces.
governance.getPolicySummary requires a specific policy UUID that must be supplied by the user.
hardRefuse.alternative: Ask the user which policy UUID to report on, then classify as T3-Insights with method: getPolicySummary and the UUID injected into the request body.

hardRefuse.reason: QueueGetResponse does not include a failure count field. SDK fields are: id, name, maxRetries, acceptsRejectedItems only.
hardRefuse.alternative: T3-Insights: use namespace: 'jobs', method: 'getTopFailures' to see processes with most failures, or request queue metrics via T3-SDK if your tenant has queue analytics enabled.
